# Photo Picker Upgrade — Design Spec

**Date:** 2026-03-10
**Status:** Approved

## Goal

Extend the photo-picker tool with two capabilities:
1. **URL log + write-back** — track uploaded blob URLs and write them into per-site image data files
2. **Component preview** — show the uploaded image rendered inside a faithful HTML mockup of the target Astro component

---

## Feature 1: URL Log + Write-Back

### Data file per site

Each app gets a new file: `apps/{site}/src/data/images.ts`

```ts
// Auto-generated by photo-picker. Edit carefully.
export const heroImages: Record<string, string> = {
  index: 'https://blob.vercel.../hero/IMG_1793.webp',
};

export const galleryImages: GalleryService[] = [
  { title: '', description: '', image: 'https://blob.vercel.../gallery/IMG_1794.webp', href: '/services/...' },
];
```

Photo-picker creates or updates this file via a new server endpoint after each upload. Pages import from it instead of hardcoding URLs inline.

**One-time page refactor** (not part of this plan): each app's `index.astro` will need `backgroundImage={heroImages.index}` instead of a hardcoded string. This is a separate task done after the tool is working.

### Hero page assignment

When `category = hero`, a "Assign to page" dropdown appears in the upload options panel. For now it only offers `index` (homepage). Future: `services`, individual service slugs.

After upload, photo-picker calls `POST /api/write-images` with `{ microsite, category, page, url }`. The server reads the existing `images.ts` (or starts fresh), updates the relevant key, and writes the file back.

### Gallery write-back

Each uploaded gallery image is appended to `galleryImages` in `images.ts` with a stub title/description (blank strings). The user fills in titles/descriptions in the file manually after generating.

### URL log (UI)

The right panel shows a running "Uploaded this session" list below the action buttons. Each entry shows:
- filename → category (and page if hero)
- blob URL (truncated, click to copy)

A "Copy all" button copies the full list as JSON. The log persists for the session in memory (no localStorage needed).

### Generate gallery button

When 4+ gallery images have been uploaded for a microsite in the current session, a "Generate gallery component" button appears at the bottom of the right panel. Clicking it:
1. Calls `GET /api/gallery-snippet?microsite={site}` which reads `images.ts` and returns a formatted `<ServicesGallery ... />` code snippet
2. Displays the snippet in a modal/overlay — user copies and pastes into their page

---

## Feature 2: Component Preview

### UI

The center panel gains two tabs: **Photo** (current behavior) and **Preview in component**. Switching tabs does not change which photo is current.

The Preview tab renders a self-contained HTML mockup (no Astro dev server needed) that changes based on the selected category:

| Category | Preview shows |
|---|---|
| `hero` | Full-width dark overlay section, headline text, 600px tall |
| `gallery` | 4-card grid (current session's gallery images for this microsite, or placeholder cards if <4) |
| `before-after`, `damage`, `repair`, `process`, `completed` | Single image card at 800×600 aspect ratio with label |
| `team` | Portrait-oriented card at 600×800 |
| `equipment` | Standard image card |

The preview uses the blob URL of the currently-uploaded (processed) image — the URL returned by the last successful upload, or the raw `/api/photos/:file` URL before upload.

---

## Architecture

### New server endpoints

| Endpoint | Description |
|---|---|
| `POST /api/write-images` | Write/update `apps/{site}/src/data/images.ts` |
| `GET /api/gallery-snippet?microsite=` | Return formatted `<ServicesGallery />` snippet from images.ts |

### `POST /api/write-images` body

```json
{
  "microsite": "deck-repair",
  "category": "hero",
  "page": "index",
  "url": "https://blob.vercel.../hero/IMG_1793.webp",
  "filename": "IMG_1793.JPG"
}
```

The server:
1. Resolves `apps/{microsite}/src/data/images.ts` relative to the monorepo root
2. Reads the existing file if it exists (regex-parses the exported objects)
3. Upserts the new URL into the correct export
4. Writes the file back with consistent formatting

### `GET /api/gallery-snippet`

Reads `images.ts` for the microsite, extracts `galleryImages`, and returns a code string:

```astro
<ServicesGallery
  heading="Our Services"
  services={[
    { title: '', description: '', image: 'https://...', href: '/services/...' },
    ...
  ]}
/>
```

### New app.js state

```js
let uploadLog = []; // { filename, category, page, url }
let sessionGalleryCount = {}; // microsite -> count of gallery uploads this session
```

After each successful upload, push to `uploadLog`, re-render the log panel, and check if gallery button should appear.

---

## Files Changed

| File | Change |
|---|---|
| `tools/photo-picker/server.js` | Add `/api/write-images`, `/api/gallery-snippet` endpoints; resolve monorepo root |
| `tools/photo-picker/public/app.js` | Add URL log state, log rendering, page assignment UI, tab switching, preview rendering, gallery button |
| `tools/photo-picker/public/index.html` | Add tab buttons, log container, gallery modal |
| `tools/photo-picker/public/style.css` | Styles for tabs, log entries, gallery modal, preview mockups |

---

## Out of Scope (this plan)

- Refactoring existing page files to import from `images.ts` (separate task)
- Hero assignment for pages other than `index`
- Before/after, team, equipment asset types beyond basic preview
- Persistence of URL log across server restarts
